title: Field Briefing: Read HTML as a Relationship Language
summary: Field Briefing: Read HTML as a Relationship Language uses an original document relationship briefing scenario to retrieve earlier HTML skills, add a bounded artifact layer, and verify transfer with source and browser evidence.
objectives: Retrieve html document boilerplate, html elements nesting, html text headings lists without step-by-step imitation.
Produce and verify a real document relationship briefing artifact or evidence report.
Preserve earlier valid requirements while diagnosing changed content and constraints.

Document relationship briefing case 1: metadata should be placed after the closing html tag so it stays invisible.

steps.title: Read the document relationship briefing contract · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 4: Which practice follows the explanation and remains valid in a changed document? Work context: document relationship briefing.
steps.why: Field Briefing: Read HTML as a Relationship Language requires this decision because each decision must preserve document meaning, user control, portability, and inspectable evidence. This is checkpoint 4 in the document relationship briefing.

content.text: document relationship briefing: A deployable HTML document declares its parsing mode, document language, character encoding, responsive viewport behavior, and a useful browser-tab title. These signals belong in precise locations: the doctype begins the source, the language belongs on the root element, metadata belongs in head, and visible content belongs in body. Elements create relationships through start tags, end tags, and nesting. Indentation makes those relationships readable to people but does not create them. A child must close before its parent closes, while void elements such as img and meta do not receive end tags. Headings label sections and express an outline; paragraphs carry prose; lists express collections or sequences; block quotations identify quoted passages; and code elements identify computer input or output. Element choice should preserve meaning when presentation is removed.
